You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

metadata.canonical_answer: At 4s, the man wearing grey pullover holds a white container with a green label; at 8s, the man holds two brown eggs over the counter; at 10s, the man begins cracking an egg shell with his thumbs over a white bowl; at 12s, the egg yolk and white fall into the bowl; at 16s, the man separates the broken eggshell halves while two yolks sit in the bowl.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

recent_thinks: [0-2] A man in a grey pullover with a 'PC' logo stands in a kitchen. He faces the camera with hands clasped. Behind him are white cabinets, a silver kettle, and a blender base. On the counter in front, a white tub and egg carton are visible. The text 'Healthy Brownies' is overlaid at the bottom left.

[2-4] The man in the grey pullover looks down at the counter, breaking eye contact with the camera. His hands unclasp as he prepares to interact with the items. The overlay text 'Healthy Brownies' persists at the bottom left. The white cabinets and appliances in the background remain stationary.

[4-6] The man lifts a white tub with green label in his right hand. A small white square bowl is now visible on the left counter. The egg carton is open, displaying brown eggs. He gestures with his left hand near the items. The 'Healthy Brownies' text overlay persists at the bottom left corner.

[6-8] The man lifts the white tub with the green label higher, presenting it directly toward the camera lens with his right hand. He shifts his gaze from the counter back to look straight at the viewer while holding the container steady. The tub features an image of brownies on the front label. The egg carton remains on the counter.

[8-10] After a visual dissolve transition, the man now holds a brown egg in each hand over a white square bowl. He looks down at the eggs, preparing to crack them. A silver metal mixing bowl has appeared on the counter behind him to the right. The white tub rests on the table surface.

[10-12] The man brings his hands together over the small white square bowl. He holds a brown egg with both hands and cracks the shell, preparing to empty the contents into the dish. The large white tub sits on the counter to his right near the egg carton.

samples.output: <think>The man cracks the brown egg over the white square bowl, pulling the shell halves apart with both hands. The egg contents fall into the dish. He holds the empty shell fragments while looking down at the bowl, which now contains the raw egg. The large white tub remains on the counter to his right.</think><answer></answer>
